subject: Public Health
title: Digital Mental Health Tools
description: A short lesson to help you apply Digital Mental Health Tools.

hook.text: There are over 20,000 mental health apps—but fewer than 5% have any clinical evidence behind them.

buildup.text: Digital tools promise therapy, mindfulness, and crisis support at scale—reaching people who can't access clinicians.

discovery.text: Some apps delivering CBT show outcomes comparable to face-to-face therapy for mild to moderate conditions.

twist.text: Data privacy is a major concern—many mental health apps sell sensitive user data to advertisers.

climax.text: Regulation is catching up slowly—quality standards for digital mental health tools are being developed.

punchline.text: Technology can scale therapy—if we ensure quality and privacy first.

quiz.question: What percentage of mental health apps have clinical evidence?
